data.content:
## Plan

Investigated all 3 sites. Each receives data that should already be
canonical; the normalize call is wasted work that re-derives fields
already present on the input.

### Site 1 — terminal-manager.ts:282 (startSessionLog)
Input: `agent: CliAgentTarget`. Per `types-agent-target.ts`,
`CliAgentTarget` extends `AgentTargetBase` which has explicit
`provider` field (canonical, populated by settings hydration in
`agent-config-normalization.ts`). Fix: `agentProvider: agent.provider`.
One-line change.

### Site 2 — terminal-manager-take-agent.ts:317 (claim audit)
Input: `claimAgent: CliAgentTarget`. The `normalized` value is used
to populate `LeaseAuditAgent` ({ provider, model, flavor, version }).
All four fields are present on `CliAgentTarget`. Fix: build the
audit-agent object directly from canonical fields. A few lines later
(:332) `toExecutionAgentInfo(claimAgent)` runs anyway — that one is
the canonical conversion for ExecutionAgentInfo, not a violation, so
leave it.

### Site 3 — terminal-manager-take-iteration.ts:357 (outcome audit)
Input: `record.agent` (type `AgentOutcomeRecord.agent` in
`agent-outcome-stats.ts:16-22`) is currently NARROWER than
`CliAgentTarget` — only `{ agentId?, label?, model?, version?, command }`.
That's why this site re-normalises: provider/flavor aren't on the
record type. Fix:
1. Widen `AgentOutcomeRecord.agent` to `{ ..., provider?, flavor? }`.
2. At record-construction time (line 208), populate provider/flavor
   from the source agent (which IS a CliAgentTarget per upstream).
3. Drop the normalize call at line 357; build LeaseAuditAgent
   directly from `record.agent.{provider,model,flavor,version}`.

### Order of work

1. Fresh worktree, `bun install --frozen-lockfile`.
2. Site 1 fix — focused commit.
3. Site 2 fix — focused commit.
4. Site 3 fix (type widen + construction + read site) — focused commit.
5. After all three: run `grep -rn "normalizeAgentIdentity" src/lib/terminal-manager*.ts` — must be zero.
6. Quality gates: lint, tsc, test, build. Fix any breakage.
7. Merge to main, push origin/main.

### Risk and rollback

Low risk — each change is local field substitution. If `record.agent`
construction site (line 208) doesn't have access to canonical
provider/flavor (e.g. came from a non-CliAgentTarget source), I
escalate by tracing back further or filing a follow-up to canonicalise
the upstream record builder.
